vocabulary.definition_ar: إنتاج الكهرباء والحرارة المفيدة في آنٍ واحد من مصدر وقود واحد
vocabulary.example_sentence: Cogeneration systems are very efficient because they use nearly all the fuel energy.

vocabulary.definition_ar: نظام اقتصادي مصمم للقضاء على النفايات من خلال إعادة الاستخدام والتدوير المستمر للمواد
vocabulary.example_sentence: The circular economy model helps companies reduce costs and environmental impact.

vocabulary.definition_ar: إزالة الكربون
vocabulary.example_sentence: Many countries plan to decarbonize their economies by 2050.

vocabulary.definition_ar: عملية تلويث شيء ما أو جعله ضاراً بإضافة مواد خطيرة
vocabulary.example_sentence: Water contamination from industrial waste is a serious environmental problem.

vocabulary.definition_ar: موزع عبر مواقع متعددة بدلاً من السيطرة من مكان مركزي واحد
vocabulary.example_sentence: A decentralized energy system uses many small power sources instead of large plants.

vocabulary.definition_ar: تحلية المياه
vocabulary.example_sentence: Saudi Arabia relies heavily on desalination for drinking water.

vocabulary.definition_ar: مزيج من مصادر الطاقة المختلفة المستخدمة لتلبية إجمالي احتياجات الطاقة
vocabulary.example_sentence: The country plans to change its energy mix to include more renewable sources.

vocabulary.definition_ar: فحص مبنى أو عملية لإيجاد طرق لتقليل استهلاك الطاقة
vocabulary.example_sentence: The factory carried out an energy audit and cut its electricity costs by a third.

vocabulary.definition_ar: سياسة تدفع لمنتجي الطاقة مقابل الكهرباء التي يُضيفونها إلى الشبكة
vocabulary.example_sentence: A feed-in tariff encouraged homeowners to install solar panels on their roofs.

vocabulary.definition_ar: أن يكون نقيضًا لشيء ما أو يتناقض معه تمامًا
vocabulary.example_sentence: Investing more in coal plants flies in the face of global climate commitments.

vocabulary.definition_ar: سند أخضر
vocabulary.example_sentence: The city issued green bonds to fund its solar energy project.

vocabulary.definition_ar: النقطة التي تكون فيها تكلفة الطاقة المتجددة مساوية للطاقة التقليدية
vocabulary.example_sentence: Solar energy has reached grid parity in many countries, making it competitive.

vocabulary.definition_ar: تفتيت الموئل الطبيعي إلى قطع أصغر غير متصلة
vocabulary.example_sentence: Habitat fragmentation makes it harder for animals to find food and mates.

vocabulary.definition_ar: مادة تستخدم لمنع مرور الحرارة أو الصوت أو الكهرباء
vocabulary.example_sentence: Good insulation in walls helps keep houses warm in winter.

vocabulary.definition_ar: دراسة الأثر البيئي الكلي لمنتج من الإنتاج إلى التخلص منه
vocabulary.example_sentence: A lifecycle assessment showed that electric cars have a lower impact over time.

vocabulary.definition_ar: السلسلة الكاملة للمراحل في وجود منتج من الإنتاج إلى التخلص منه
vocabulary.example_sentence: Companies must consider the environmental impact throughout a product's entire lifecycle.

vocabulary.definition_ar: استخدام تدفق الهواء وتصميم المبنى لتبريد المساحات دون أنظمة ميكانيكية
vocabulary.example_sentence: Natural ventilation reduces the need for air conditioning in warm climates.

vocabulary.definition_ar: بلاستيك دقيق
vocabulary.example_sentence: Microplastics have been found in drinking water worldwide.

vocabulary.definition_ar: نوع من الأراضي الرطبة تراكمت فيها طبقات من النباتات الميتة على مدى آلاف السنين
vocabulary.example_sentence: Peatlands store more carbon than all the world's forests combined.

vocabulary.definition_ar: ترقُّق طبقة الأوزون التي تحمي الأرض من الأشعة فوق البنفسجية
vocabulary.example_sentence: Ozone depletion caused by certain chemicals increased skin cancer rates.

vocabulary.definition_ar: تربة صقيعية
vocabulary.example_sentence: Melting permafrost releases methane gas into the atmosphere.

vocabulary.definition_ar: كهروضوئي
vocabulary.example_sentence: Photovoltaic cells convert sunlight directly into electricity.

vocabulary.definition_ar: أن تكون من الرواد في تطوير أو استخدام أساليب أو مجالات معرفة جديدة
vocabulary.example_sentence: Denmark pioneered the use of wind energy in the 1970s.

vocabulary.definition_ar: شخص ينتج الطاقة ويستهلكها في نفس الوقت، مثلًا بالألواح الشمسية
vocabulary.example_sentence: As a prosumer, she sells excess solar electricity back to the national grid.

vocabulary.definition_ar: مقياس لمقدار تغيير عامل ما لتوازن طاقة الأرض
vocabulary.example_sentence: Scientists use radiative forcing to compare the warming effect of different gases.

vocabulary.definition_ar: إعادة التشجير
vocabulary.example_sentence: The government has started a reforestation program in the desert.

vocabulary.definition_ar: إضافة ميزات جديدة أو محسنة لمبنى أو نظام موجود
vocabulary.example_sentence: They decided to retrofit the old building with modern energy-saving technology.

vocabulary.definition_ar: شبكة كهرباء تستخدم التكنولوجيا الرقمية لمراقبة وإدارة تدفق الطاقة بكفاءة
vocabulary.example_sentence: The smart grid automatically adjusts power distribution based on real-time demand.

vocabulary.definition_ar: أصل فقد قيمته الاقتصادية في وقت أبكر مما كان متوقعًا بسبب تحولات الطاقة
vocabulary.example_sentence: Coal power plants risk becoming stranded assets as clean energy expands.

vocabulary.definition_ar: أموال تقدمها الحكومة للمساعدة في تقليل تكلفة شيء ما
vocabulary.example_sentence: The government offers subsidies for people who install solar panels.

vocabulary.definition_ar: القدرة على الحفاظ على عملية ما أو دعمها بشكل مستمر دون استنزاف الموارد
vocabulary.example_sentence: The company focuses on sustainability by using renewable materials in all products.

vocabulary.definition_ar: الوصول إلى مورد أو مصدر طاقة والاستفادة منه
vocabulary.example_sentence: Iceland taps into geothermal energy from underground hot springs.

vocabulary.definition_ar: قدرة مادة على امتصاص طاقة الحرارة وتخزينها
vocabulary.example_sentence: Thick stone walls provide thermal mass that keeps buildings cool during the day.

vocabulary.definition_ar: الكهرباء المولدة باستخدام حركة المد والجزر في المحيط
vocabulary.example_sentence: Coastal areas with strong tides are ideal locations for tidal power plants.

vocabulary.definition_ar: التغيير التدريجي من نظام أو طريقة أو مصدر طاقة إلى آخر
vocabulary.example_sentence: Many companies are transitioning to renewable energy sources for their operations.

vocabulary.definition_ar: ترتيب خيارات إدارة النفايات من الأكثر إلى الأقل تفضيلًا بيئيًا
vocabulary.example_sentence: The waste hierarchy puts reduction above recycling as the best option.

vocabulary.definition_ar: الطاقة المولدة باستخدام الرياح لتدوير التوربينات التي تنتج الكهرباء
vocabulary.example_sentence: Wind energy is becoming cheaper and more popular in many countries.

vocabulary.definition_ar: تقليل الاعتماد على شيء ما تدريجيًا حتى لا تعود هناك حاجة إليه
vocabulary.example_sentence: Countries must wean themselves off fossil fuels to slow global warming.

vocabulary.definition_ar: الكهرباء المولَّدة باستخدام توربينات الرياح لتحويل الطاقة الحركية للرياح إلى طاقة كهربائية.
vocabulary.example_sentence: Offshore wind power capacity has grown rapidly as turbine technology has improved.

paragraphs: Saudi Arabia, a nation historically *synonymous* with oil production, is now leading one of the world's most ambitious renewable energy transformations. Under the Vision 2030 initiative, the Kingdom has *committed* to generating 50% of its electricity from renewable sources by 2030, fundamentally *shifting* its energy paradigm from fossil fuels to clean alternatives.

The NEOM project, located in the northwest region of Tabuk, exemplifies this transformation. Scientists have reported that this futuristic city will be entirely powered by renewable energy, with solar panels covering vast desert areas and wind turbines *harnessing* the Red Sea's coastal winds. The project's planners *estimate* that NEOM will produce enough clean energy to power 1.5 million homes while creating thousands of jobs for young Saudi professionals. If successful, NEOM could serve as a *blueprint* for other nations seeking to *transition* from oil-dependent economies to sustainable energy systems.

Solar energy presents particularly promising opportunities in Saudi Arabia, where researchers have documented that certain regions receive over 2,500 hours of sunshine annually. The King Salman Renewable Energy Initiative has already *allocated* billions of dollars to construct solar farms across the Kingdom's desert landscapes. These installations *utilize* advanced photovoltaic technology that can operate efficiently even in extreme heat conditions. Energy experts predict that by 2025, Saudi Arabia will *rank among* the world's top ten solar energy producers.

The renewable energy sector is also *fostering* innovation in education and research. King Abdullah University of Science and Technology (KAUST) has established cutting-edge laboratories where students and researchers *collaborate* on developing more efficient solar cells and energy storage solutions. Women now *comprise* approximately 35% of renewable energy engineering graduates, reflecting the Kingdom's commitment to gender diversity in STEM fields. These graduates often *pursue* careers with international companies that are establishing regional headquarters in Riyadh and Jeddah.

As Saudi Arabia continues this energy revolution, the implications extend far beyond national borders. The Kingdom's success in renewable energy could inspire other Gulf nations to adopt similar strategies, potentially transforming the entire Middle East into a global hub for clean energy production and technological innovation.

In the heart of the Arabian Peninsula, where temperatures can *soar* above 50 degrees Celsius, Saudi Arabia is *undertaking* one of the world's most ambitious renewable energy transformations. The Kingdom's Vision 2030 program has set a target to generate 50% of its electricity from renewable sources by 2030, a goal that seemed impossible just a decade ago but now appears increasingly *feasible*.

The centerpiece of this transformation is the Saudi Green Initiative, which *encompasses* massive solar and wind projects across the country's vast desert landscape. The most impressive project is the Mohammed bin Rashid Al Maktoum Solar Park extension into Saudi territory, where engineers have had to *devise* innovative solutions to combat the extreme heat that can actually reduce solar panel efficiency. "We had to *think outside the box* when designing these installations," reported Dr. Sarah Al-Mahmoud, lead engineer at the Saudi Renewable Energy Program Development Office. "The desert presents unique challenges, but it also offers *unprecedented* opportunities for solar energy generation."

What makes Saudi Arabia's approach particularly innovative is how the Kingdom has managed to *turn the tables* on traditional energy paradigms. Rather than viewing the harsh desert climate as an obstacle, Saudi engineers have learned to *capitalize on* these conditions. Advanced cooling systems and specialized panel materials have been developed to maintain efficiency even when ambient temperatures *fluctuate* dramatically. The results have been remarkable: Saudi solar installations now *yield* up to 30% more energy per square meter than similar projects in temperate climates, according to recent data from the King Abdullah City for Atomic and Renewable Energy.

The economic implications of this renewable energy boom cannot be *understated*. Industry experts estimate that Saudi Arabia's renewable sector will create over 750,000 jobs by 2030, providing new career opportunities for the Kingdom's growing population of young professionals. Moreover, the government has announced plans to *phase out* oil dependency for domestic electricity generation entirely by 2040, allowing more petroleum exports and diversifying the national economy. Local communities in remote areas have already begun to benefit from *off-grid* solar installations that provide reliable electricity for the first time in many regions.

As Saudi Arabia continues to *scale up* its renewable energy infrastructure, the Kingdom is positioning itself as a global leader in desert solar technology. International delegations regularly visit Saudi installations to study the innovative approaches that have made large-scale renewable energy viable in one of the world's most challenging climates, proving that environmental obstacles can become competitive advantages with the right vision and technology.

sections.content_ar: الصوت المبني للمجهول في الماضي يستخدم للتركيز على الفعل أو الشيء المتأثر بالفعل وليس الفاعل. نستخدم was/were + التصريف الثالث للفعل
sections.content_en:
The passive voice (past) is used when we want to focus on the action or the person/thing that received the action, rather than who did it.

In passive voice, the object of an active sentence becomes the subject. We use was/were + past participle to form the past passive.

When to use:
• When the doer is unknown or not important
• When we want to emphasize what happened to something
• In formal writing and reports

Structure: Subject + was/were + past participle (+ by + agent)

sections.content: Subject + was/were + past participle (+ by + agent)

items.sentence: The solar panels were installed last month.
items.translation_ar: تم تركيب الألواح الشمسية الشهر الماضي

items.sentence: Wind energy was discovered centuries ago.
items.translation_ar: تم اكتشاف طاقة الرياح منذ قرون

items.sentence: The hydroelectric dam was built by the government in 2015.
items.translation_ar: تم بناء السد الكهرومائي من قبل الحكومة في 2015

items.correct: The solar panels were installed yesterday.
items.explanation_ar: نحتاج was/were مع التصريف الثالث في الماضي المبني للمجهول

items.wrong: Wind turbines was constructed last year.
items.correct: Wind turbines were constructed last year.
items.explanation_ar: نستخدم were مع الجمع وليس was
نستخدم التصريف الثالث built وليس building في المبني للمجهول
